Description: Eutreptiella sp. 'Long Island Sound 2002' genome assembly, chromosome: 11
Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Discoba; Euglenozoa; Euglenida; Spirocuta; Euglenophyceae; Eutreptiales; Eutreptiaceae; Eutreptiella
Assembly: The assembly peEutLong1.2 is based on 50x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Aquatic Symbiosis Genomics Project (https://www.aquaticsymbiosisgenomics.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and plastid genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 3 (0 - 10.3 Mbp) and chromosome 5 (39.1 - 52.9 Mbp) are unknown.

Assembly: This project presents the diploid genome assembly of the European greenfinch, Chloris Chloris, from a sample provided by Jan T. Lifjeld. The data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). The bChlchl1.1 assembly is based on 29x PacBio HiFi and 76x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt and assembled with hifiasm together with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies, ensuring that reads for hap1 scaffolding did not contain k-mers unique to hap2, and vice versa. YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes, as confirmed by Hi-C data, are named according to size. Both sex chromosomes have been included in hap1.

The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt and assembled with hifiasm together with Hi-C data to generate a pair of haplotype-resolved assemblies. K-mers from the two assemblies were used to filter Hi-C reads to create two sets of reads for scaffolding the assemblies, ensuring that reads for hap1 scaffolding did not contain k-mers unique to hap2, and vice versa. YaHS was used for scaffolding. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes, as confirmed by Hi-C data, are named according to size. Both sex chromosomes have been included in hap1.

Assembly: The assembly bVanVan1.hap1.1 is based on 48x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome Z identified by alignment to GCA_017639485.1.
